segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Then there were the red baby shoes.
segments.zh: 然后还有那双红色的婴儿鞋。

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Yeah – weren't they split?
segments.zh: 是啊——它们不是裂开了吗？

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: They still managed to ship them overseas, though.
segments.zh: 不过他们还是设法把它们运到了海外。

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: That's another area we need to discuss.
segments.zh: 那是我们需要讨论的另一个方面。

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: It's not as simple as you first think, and we can show that by taking a very different approach to it.
segments.zh: 它并不像你最初想的那么简单，我们可以通过采用一种非常不同的方法来证明这一点。

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: Absolutely.
segments.zh: 完全正确。那么我们来讨论一下……

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: So let's discuss ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For my project on invertebrates, I chose to study tardigrades.
segments.zh: 在我的无脊椎动物项目中，我选择了研究缓步动物。这些是微观的——或者更准确地说——是近乎微观的动物。已知的这类微小动物有超过一千种，属于缓步动物门。大多数缓步动物的长度在0.05到1毫米之间，尽管最大的物种可以长到1.2毫米长。它们有时也被称为“水熊虫”：“水”是因为它们最适宜在水中生存，“熊”是因为它们移动的方式。“苔藓猪仔”是缓步动物的另一个名字，因为从正面看它们的样子。它们于1773年由约翰·格策在德国首次发现，他创造了“缓步动物”这个名字。

words.def: 微观的，用显微镜才能看到的

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These are microscopic – or to be more precise – near-microscopic animals.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are well over a thousand known species of these tiny animals, which belong to the phylum Tardigrada.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Most tardigrades range in length from 0.05 to 1 millimetre, though the largest species can grow to be 1.2 millimetres in length.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They are also sometimes called 'water bears': 'water' because that's where they thrive best, and 'bear' because of the way they move.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: 'Moss piglet' is another name for tardigrades because of the way they look when viewed from the front.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They were first discovered in Germany in 1773 by Johann Goeze, who coined the name Tardigrada.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As I say, there are many different species of tardigrade – too many to describe here – but, generally speaking, the different species share similar physical traits.
segments.zh: 正如我所说，缓步动物有很多不同的种类——这里无法一一描述——但总的来说，不同种类具有相似的物理特征。它们的身体短而圆——有点像桶——身体由四个体节组成。每个体节有一对腿，腿的末端有四到八个锋利的爪子。我还应该提到，有些物种没有爪子；它们有的是吸盘，通过吸力起作用。这些吸盘使缓步动物能够附着在表面上或抓住猎物。在体内，没有肺或任何呼吸器官。相反，氧气和血液在充满体腔的液体中运输。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They have a body which is short, and also rounded – a bit like a barrel – and the body comprises four segments.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Each segment has a pair of legs, at the end of which are between four and eight sharp claws.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I should also say that some species don't have any claws; what they have are discs, and these work by means of suction.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They enable the tardigrade to cling on to surfaces or to grip its prey.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Within the body, there are no lungs, or any organs for breathing at all.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Instead, oxygen and also blood are transported in a fluid that fills the cavity of the body.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As far as the tardigrade's head is concerned, the best way I can describe this is that it looks rather strange – a bit squashed even – though many of the websites I looked at described its appearance as cute, which isn't exactly very scientific.
segments.zh: 说到水熊虫的头部，我能想到的最好描述就是它看起来相当奇怪——甚至有点扁塌塌的——尽管我浏览的很多网站都说它长得可爱，但这显然不太科学。水熊虫的嘴巴是一种可以向外张开的管子，里面露出像牙齿一样的结构，叫做‘口针’。这些口针非常锋利，足以刺穿植物或动物细胞。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The tardigrade's mouth is a kind of tube that can open outwards to reveal teeth-like structures known as 'stylets'.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These are sharp enough to pierce plant or animal cells.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, where are tardigrades found?
segments.zh: 那么，水熊虫在哪里能找到呢？它们遍布世界各地，栖息在各种环境中：最常见的是在湖床上、多种植物上或非常潮湿的环境里。一些有趣的研究发现，水熊虫能够承受辐射和极高的压力，还能忍受低至零下200摄氏度或高达148摄氏度以上的温度，这简直热得不可思议。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, they live in every part of the world, in a variety of habitats: most commonly, on the bed of a lake, or on many kinds of plants or in very wet environments.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There's been some interesting research which has found that tardigrades are capable of surviving radiation and very high pressure, and they're also able to withstand temperatures as cold as –200 degrees centigrade, or highs of more than 148 degrees centigrade, which is incredibly hot.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It has been said that tardigrades could survive long after human beings have been wiped out, even in the event of an asteroid hitting the earth.
segments.zh: 据说，即使人类灭绝很久之后，水熊虫也能存活下来，哪怕是小行星撞击地球。如果环境变得过于极端，水熊虫面临干涸的危险，它们就会进入一种叫做‘隐生’的状态。它们缩回头和腿，卷成一个球，称为‘tun’（拼写为T-U-N），新陈代谢降至正常水平的1%以下。它们可以保持这种状态，直到重新遇到水，然后在几小时内复活。在隐生状态下，水熊虫会产生一种保护DNA的蛋白质。2016年，科学家复活了两只已经处于tun状态超过30年的水熊虫。有报道称，1948年曾有一只120岁的tun被复活，但这个实验从未被重复过。目前太空中正在进行几项测试，以确定水熊虫能在那里存活多久。据我所知，目前的记录是10天。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If conditions become too extreme and tardigrades are at risk of drying out, they enter a state called cryptobiosis.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They curl into a ball, called a tun – that's T-U-N – by retracting their head and legs, and their metabolism drops to less than one percent of normal levels.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They can remain like this until they are re-introduced to water, when they will come back to life in a matter of a few hours.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: While in a state of cryptobiosis, tardigrades produce a protein that protects their DNA.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In 2016, scientists revived two tardigrades that had been tuns for more than 30 years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There was a report that, in 1948, a 120-year-old tun was revived, but this experiment has never been repeated.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are currently several tests taking place in space, to determine how long tardigrades might be able to survive there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I believe the record so far is 10 days.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, ermm, moving on.
segments.zh: 那么，呃，我们继续。关于它们的饮食，水熊虫靠摄取液体生存。虽然它们有牙齿，但不用来咀嚼。它们吸食苔藓的汁液，或从海藻中提取液体，但有些物种会捕食其他物种或同类的其他水熊虫。我想这并不奇怪，因为水熊虫主要由液体构成，并且表面覆盖着一层凝胶。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In terms of their diet, tardigrades consume liquids in order to survive.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Although they have teeth, they don't use these for chewing.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They suck the juices from moss, or extract fluid from seaweed, but some species prey on other tardigrades, from other species or within their own.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I suppose this isn't surprising, given that tardigrades are mainly comprised of liquid and are coated with a type of gel.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Finally, I'd like to mention the conservation status of tardigrades.
segments.zh: 最后，我想提一下水熊虫的保护状况。据估计，它们已经存在了大约5亿年，在此期间，它们经历了五次大灭绝。所以，水熊虫没有被国际自然保护联盟评估，也不在任何濒危物种名单上，这大概不会让你们感到惊讶。一些研究人员称它们正在繁盛。大家有什么问题想问吗？

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It is estimated that they have been in existence for approximately half a billion years and, in that time, they have survived five mass extinctions.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, it will probably come as no surprise to you, that tardigrades have not been evaluated by the International Union for Conservation of Nature and are not on any endangered list.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some researchers have described them as thriving.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Does anyone have any questions they'd like to ask?

items.prompt: Tardigrades: also known as water 'bears' (due to how they 31 ______) and 'moss piglets'

pairs.note: 题干中'how they move'对应原文'the way they move'，完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问水熊虫得名'water bears'的原因，定位句明确说'bear because of the way they move'，所以答案就是'move'。

items.prompt: a 32 ______ round body and four pairs of legs

pairs.note: 题干中32是题号，原文直接出现short，对应填空处。
round和rounded是同义词，都表示圆形。

pairs.p: four segments. Each segment has a pair of legs
pairs.note: 原文用四个体节每节一对腿来表达四对腿，是释义。

paraphrase.explain: 题干描述身体特征，原文先提到身体short and rounded，然后说身体有四个体节，每节有一对腿，对应四对腿。

pairs.p: claws; what they have are discs
pairs.note: 原文说有些物种没有爪子，而是有discs，与题干claws or ___对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问用于抓握的爪子或什么，原文提到有些物种没有爪子，而是有discs，通过吸力工作，因此答案应为discs。

items.prompt: body filled with a liquid that carries both 34 ______ and blood

pairs.q: body filled with a liquid that carries both
pairs.p: oxygen and also blood are transported in a fluid that fills the cavity of the body
pairs.note: 题干说液体携带两种东西，原文说氧气和血液被液体运输，结构相同但语序调整。
题干填空处对应原文的oxygen，直接匹配。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'oxygen and also blood are transported in a fluid that fills the cavity of the body'，与题干'body filled with a liquid that carries both... and blood'对应，因此答案就是oxygen。

items.prompt: mouth shaped like a 35 ______ with teeth called stylets

pairs.note: 题干用'shaped like a'描述形状，原文用'is a kind of'说明类别，都是说嘴像管子。
题干和原文都直接出现'tube'，完全一致。

pairs.p: to reveal teeth-like structures known as 'stylets'
pairs.note: 题干说'with teeth called stylets'，原文说'teeth-like structures known as stylets'，意思相同，只是表达方式不同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问嘴像什么，原文说嘴是一种管子，并且后面提到牙齿状结构叫stylet，所以答案就是tube。

items.prompt: very resilient and can exist in very low or high 36 ______

pairs.note: resilient 与 able to withstand 同义，都表示'能承受'
原文直接出现 temperatures，是答案词

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 tardigrades 能承受极低和极高温度，与题干 'very resilient and can exist in very low or high temperatures' 对应，答案词 temperatures 在原文中出现。

items.prompt: A type of 37 ______ ensures their DNA is not damaged.

pairs.note: 题干中的 'a type of' 对应原文中的 'a protein'，表示一种蛋白质。

pairs.q: ensures their DNA is not damaged
pairs.note: 题干中的 'ensures their DNA is not damaged' 对应原文中的 'protects their DNA'，都是保护DNA的意思。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'tardigrades produce a protein that protects their DNA'，其中 'protein' 是答案，'protects their DNA' 与题干 'ensures their DNA is not damaged' 同义。

items.prompt: Research is underway to find out how many days they can stay alive in 38 ______

pairs.note: how many days 被替换为 how long，意思相同。
stay alive 被替换为 survive，都是‘存活’的意思。
原文直接出现 in space，对应题干中的 space。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问‘在太空中能存活多少天’，定位句提到‘there are currently several tests taking place in space, to determine how long tardigrades might be able to survive there’，其中 in space 直接对应 space，how long 对应 how many days，survive 对应 stay alive。

items.prompt: consume liquids, e.g., those found in moss or 39 ______

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。
题干中'those found in moss or'对应原文'moss, or'，注意逗号。
答案'seaweed'在原文中直接出现，与题干'39 ______'对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'consume liquids'和'moss'在原文中直接出现，答案'seaweed'紧随其后，通过定位这些关键词即可找到答案。

items.prompt: They are not considered to be 40 ______

pairs.note: 题干'not considered to be'与原文'not on any'都表示否定，但措辞不同。
题干中的数字40是题号，答案词直接对应原文中的'endangered'。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'不被认为是____'，定位句说'not on any endangered list'，其中'endangered'就是答案。通过否定词'not'和'endangered'定位。
